Visiting the Louvre Museum is truly a memorable experience—a must for anyone coming to Paris. But preparation is everything: tickets should be booked well in advance, and choosing the earliest time slot helps you avoid the huge crowds that start lining up before the doors even open.

Because the museum is enormous, it’s worth studying the map and planning your route ahead of time. There are also many apps available to help you quickly find the iconic masterpieces if your time is limited. If that’s your case, go straight to the Mona Lisa—and don’t miss the nearby sculptures around her. For true museum lovers, though, give yourself plenty of time; the Louvre is vast and deserves slow exploration.

At the exit, you’ll find museum shops and a few famous cafés. The shops are better for souvenirs than for art—don’t expect rolled protective packaging for paintings or prints, and options are limited. Drinks and snacks are available in the cafés, but note that coffee must be consumed in designated areas (no wandering the halls with it). Water bottles are allowed, and pastries make a nice break between galleries.

In short, plan ahead, pace yourself, and expect to leave with memories—not necessarily a painting under your arm.

Booked our tickets months in advance & highly recommend doing so. We had booked thefirst session & unfortunately had a 2 hour wait in the cold & light rain due to an issue in the Louvre. They had a plumbing leak overnight & that delayed the opening. There was no communication from any of the staff while we were all waiting in the line to let us know what the delays were. We found out online while waiting which was super frustrating.
Once inside the water damaged wing was closed off which was fine but so was the area that held the Royal Jewels as the were robbed a few months ago. That was a shame as we thought that wing would have reopened.
Apart from that we spent many hours wandering from room to room & it was amazingly beautiful. It was my 2nd visit & I spent longer here with my kids this time.
Really enjoyed it & highly recomend it.
Our 16 & 18 year old kids also loved it
World famous museum in the Paris. You can spending time all day here if you love arts and history. There has many things to see not only Mona Lisa.

it’s very crowded with visitors, i didn’t go inside because the ticket is very expensive, and also the line is long, many people want to go in here hehe, but it’s still nice to take photos from the outside, and there are some quiet spots too. oh yeah here you also have to stay careful with strangers who approach you

Breathtakingly beautiful.
Well worth a visit. Get there arealy and book. The gardens were an extra bonus and great for grabbing a coffee and chilling

What an awesome day at the Louvre! We pre planned our itinerary for all the major exhibits we wanted to see and our day went super smooth. We went on a Monday and had a great experience. The Apartment of Napoleon Bonaparte III was awesome, we are glad we included it in our Itinerary.
Fairly busy inside but we didnt have to queue to get in. Bought our tickets once there.  Great museum.
Definitely get online tickets for a time slot.  There was a delayed opening.  Those who just got there couldn’t get in.

I visited the Louvre Museum and had a wonderful experience. The art collections are stunning, especially the Mona Lisa. The atmosphere is rich with history and beauty. I loved every moment and highly recommend it to anyone visiting Paris. A must-see for art and culture lovers!

Buy tickets in advance even in the summer, it's very crowded, but if you enjoy art and are happy to walk around and look at the less popular items, you can get lost in a practically empty museum. We walked way over to the Napoleon apartment and other parts of the Louvre and they were a lot less people than we're standing in front of the Mona. Lisa. They had a fabulous couture dress collection going on in parts of the museum that were absolutely breathtaking to look at. Really enjoyed looking at the building itself and not just the artwork which is a masterpiece as well. When you're walking around some of the windows, if you look outside you can see beautiful statues and the grounds surrounding the building all framed lovely in those big picture windows. I probably could have spent a week walking around this place. Looking at everything and enjoying this amazing museum, highly recommend you buy tickets in advance.

Absolutely stunning, would recommend. Very busy during the sunny weeks. Lots to see, can make an entire day out of it, and tickets were very cheap.

WORD OF ADVICE: Make sure to bring sunglasses, as everything is white so all the sunlight gets reflected directly into your eyes, making it hard to see.

Book your tickets ahead of time to avoid the long lines. Get a guided tour to see the main art pieces, the place is huge and overwhelming. You can easily spend the entire day and still wouldn't finish even half of the museum.

Obviously, a major landmark in Paris. A must see.

The museum is huge with several levels and it will take you hours or days depending on how "thorough" you are.

I highly recommend you buy your ticket in advance - at least 2 weeks so that the day AND time you want are available.

World class museum; staff can be difficult if you don’t speak French, very unfortunate.

If you don’t buy tickets in advance, or they’re sold out online, there is still an opportunity to buy and enter through a separate cue!

A great museum. Good structure. Good service. A day is not enough to visit everything. Recommend buying the tickets in advance and getting there on time. Nothing to complain.

The museum has expositions from a variety of fields. Asian, Egyptian, Italian, Greek among others cultures are shown.

Huge like you wouldn’t believe!! The moment you walk into the grounds you feel the enormous scale.

You cannot buy tickets at the museum and must purchase them in advance online, usually 24-48hrs ahead of when you want to visit.

The museum is popular all year round, but peak summer is probably the height of tourist crowds.

You need a full day really if you want to look at as much as possible. Still you would be better off planning ahead of time visiting which exhibits you must see vs any you could sacrifice for time.

The most beautiful thing I've ever seen was the Venus de Milo. All works and paintings are beautiful; the power of art is always striking, but it's especially striking when it has survived through the centuries without losing its beauty or relevance.

What an amazing museum filled with incredible art and rich history. This place is a a must see for anyone visiting. With such an intensive collection, it would take over 3 months to truly see every single piece. I highly recommend going with a tour guide to help navigate and make most of your visit through this magnificent museum.

Great to see, obviously very busy but take your time and wander about and see everything. Weather was poor on the day we visited but still was great to look around. So much to see and the gardens surrounding are lovely to visit and watch the world go by.

Amazing museum, full of history and beauty. I definitely recommend visiting. Tickets should be purchased in advance. You can easily spend hours looking at paintings and artifacts, and the best part is that everyone can find something they love. Especially the Mona Lisa — a small painting in the middle of a large hall, surrounded by 300 visitors. If you're traveling to Paris, make sure to visit the Louvre! 👍

I feel incredibly lucky to have added such an experience to my life. Seeing the Mona Lisa with my own eyes is certainly a unique experience, but I don't think you should overlook all these magnificent works just for the Mona Lisa. Every room, every work, holds a unique story. It might be a lengthy trip, but I'd love to come back and see each work in its own different details.
Absolutely amazing experience. Not enough time to see everything. I'd recommend a few days to get the most out of it. There is simply too much to see.

I visited the Louvre — a place that feels less like a museum and more like a condensed journey through human history. You enter through the former royal palace of France and walk into corridors where civilizations sit side by side: Ancient Egypt with its mysterious statues and inscriptions; Ancient Persia, with Achaemenid reliefs and artifacts that reflect order, power, and grandeur; Greece with its balance and ideal beauty; and Rome with its sense of scale and realism.

In the European art sections, the paintings tell the story of centuries of thought, religion, and humanity, and the Mona Lisa — despite its small size — acts like a focal point drawing every gaze. The Louvre is vast and rich in detail; after a few hours, you realize it can never be fully seen in one visit. It ultimately reminds you that civilizations are not separate, but part of a continuous chain, each adding something to our shared human memory.

The Louvre is a great place and you can find museums there like the moralizing or the jewelry the museum is huge but I recommend buying tickets on site on the internet you can't buy them if you don't have a French tariff
Plan to spend 3 to 4 hours. There are a lot of steps, so pace yourself. Highlights for us were the Mona Lisa, Greek sculptures, Michelangelo + Rodin, Venus de Milo and just the whole experience.

This place has turned into a racket, watch out, NOT ONE MENTION of their closed wings when buying tickets. You pay FULL PRICE and enjoy two thirds of what you saw one year ago. CHECK THE QR CODE FOR ROOM AVAILABILITY. ESPECIALLY IF YOU WANT TO SEE SPECIFIC ART PIECES!!

No need to buy the “guided tour” if you’re just going for an in and out experience, like checking just the most iconic art pieces for example would be a lot easier and faster if you just get your time-framed access and you walk at your own pace and literally every inch from the inside it’s just amazing … in the other end, if you consider yourself a genuine art lover well get then the guided tour where they explain the background of the master pieces.

This is an institution! They do a great job on line control and once you enter the place flows nicely.  The Mona Lisa is the most popular piece and the top tip is to stay in line to see it until you can get to front.  They usher the people out and to the left in the front. There is a guard that counts to 3 and then starts to herb people to exit in the front. So remember no turning back and you will get front row view of this masterpiece!!!! Once you get to see the masterpieces winged victory etc… The rest of the art isn’t as interesting as more recent art pieces (d’orsay!).   But extraordinary and if you find the carousel area its a surprise to see the selections of stores down there.

Great art pieces brought together , benches are available to sit in the lobbies which helps to relax the legs in the long and tiring trip inside the museum , except the mona lisa you will get ample amount of time and space to enjoy other art pieces

Love love love this place, i stayed 5 hours and it wasn’t enough. I recommend doing a full day there, but beware that the cafe inside is expensive and they don’t serve hot beverages after 16hs.

I wish I had been able to come on the first weekend of the month for the free entry but oh well, next time ♥️

Want an experience that is one of the greatest, amazing place to see art and architecture, this is the place, only downfall is not having adequate seating places for customers, walked 27,000 steps 17 flights of stairs and few seating areas and seats were awkward and uncomfortable, Amazing experience though 😀

The Louvre is one of those places in Paris that you really cannot skip. It is one of the most important museums in Europe, and also one of those rare places where the scale of it actually adds to the experience. It is huge, so realistically you will not see everything in one visit, but that almost feels like part of the point. It gives you a reason to come back.

Inside, you have some of the most famous works in the world, like the Mona Lisa, the Venus de Milo and the Winged Victory of Samothrace. The architecture, the palace rooms and the glass pyramid make the whole experience feel iconic even before you start looking at the collection. It is one of those places where the building is already part of the art. Just make sure to book in advance and go with a bit of patience, because it is usually very full and queues are just part of it. Very worth it, especially on a first visit to Paris.

Be prepared to spend alot of time here, after all, it IS the largest museum in the world.  I'm not sure that even several months would be enough time!  The earlier you go the better.  Line ups can be long.  Of course, you will want to see the Mona Lisa, but there are MANY other fantastic exhibits like the Hammurabi Code, Venus de Milo, the Crouching Scribe and the Marriage of Cana......to name a few. It's a museum experience like none other!!

This museum taught me a hard lesson: don’t be too kind when someone asks you to take their photo — it will cost you your spot and your time. The suited security guards showed no courtesy to tourists whatsoever. Without any queue system or warning, they simply rushed anyone who looked like a tourist away from the Mona Lisa after barely a minute, leaving no time to take in the painting yourself.
Worth visiting once, but the staff’s poor attitude means I won’t be returning.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Was Absolutely Amazing, if you like art and history, this is the place to vist, but you have to plan ahead as there is too much to see, makes sure you try avoid busy times as the foot fall is crazy, trying to see the Mona Lisa is a experience ppl where going wild to take a pic lol, but key is planning each floor to see is a full day vist if you plan to see everything.

A lot of amazing exhibits. The building itself is also pretty cool. Try to arrive early if you want to get through must of the exhibits. We arrived in the afternoon and could only get through a few.

If you want to see the Mona Lisa, go directly to the Denon section. The signs take you through the sync your all around the museum.
Long wait time to get in and use the washrooms. Also extremely busy. However, the museum is gorgeous and has some must see art work.

A truly amazing museum where to spend a whole day! Unfortunately the audio guide wasn’t available when we visited, but all key information are available next to paintings or can be browsed online.

Paintings are easy to find with clear visual instructions! The only room I found particularly crowded was were the Mona Lisa is displayed. But with some patience, you’re able to get to the front row in no time !

There are many snack bars and restaurants inside the museum that offer a wide selection of food and drinks.
I love it. Been twice, plan to visit more. But just for artworks. The whole inside thing is like impossible escape room

It is a world of beauty. If you like or not painting. You will find something to blow your mind. Just the infrastructure himself significant. It is sad to fund out all the area where the crowds and jellewry were are no longer available to see. But is so much to see all over the museum.

Easy to get there by public transport. Offers lockers with no cost. Impossible to get to know there whole
place in one visit. Worth to hire an audio guide.

Definitely buy tickets in advance. If you are a student in the EU under 26, you can get in for free (you still need a reservation). Don't forget your student ID card!

The museum was very crowded, but that's to be expected. Don't expect you'll see the Mona Lisa up close. If you want to snap a quick photo of it, you can take one of the paths on the side, but you are not allowed to stop, so you have to do it while walking.

The rest of the most famous works were not as crowded, so you could get to the front row quickly. Just don't expect to get a picture without other visitors in the frame.

We had a reservation for 13:30 and ended up entering around an hour later. If you are a quick walker, that might be enough time, but it sadly wasn't enough for us.

The giftshop and the bookstore closes at 18:30. The staff was very helpful. If the line at the checkout is too long, there are more registers upstairs in the bookstore section.

The Louvre exhibits sculptures, objets d'art, paintings, drawings, and archaeological finds. At any given point in time, approximately 38,000 objects from prehistory to the 21st century are being exhibited over an area of 72,735 m2 (782,910 sq ft), making it the largest museum in the world.

Really enjoyed seeing some of the most famed art pieces on the world. I would suggest prebooking a timeslot and coming early. Still a lot of free cupboards for jackets and backpacks if you arrive early. The whole experience was really rememberable.
Beautiful, historic place. It is always super crowded but theres nothing to do about it. You can spend hours in there, and Entry is free for youth EU residents!

An incredible collection of artefacts from across the world. It's huge and you need at least a few days to view everything.
Recommend booking tickets in advance to ensure entry and avoiding the large queues.
The Mona Lisa attracts huge queues, however, the Feast at Cana is on the opposite wall which is far more impressive.
Not great signage inside, so it does take time to find where you are and navigate to the items you want to see.
Great bakery inside and the restaurant is decent too, although expensive.
Free for under 25s is a nice touch

⭐ 5/5 — Iconic, Romantic, and Absolutely Magical at Night

Even without going inside, visiting Louvre Museum was an unforgettable experience. I didn’t enter the museum itself, but simply being outside and seeing the Louvre Pyramid was more than enough to understand why this place is so iconic. At night, the pyramid lights up beautifully, reflecting off the glass and surrounding structures in a way that feels elegant and timeless.

We visited while it was raining, and instead of taking away from the experience, it actually made it more magical. The reflections of the lights on the wet ground created a romantic atmosphere, calm and cinematic — truly one of those “Paris moments.”

The lighting, the symmetry, and the contrast between the modern glass pyramid and the historic buildings around it are stunning. It’s beautiful, peaceful, and captivating, even without stepping inside the museum.

A must-visit place in Paris, especially at night.
Iconic, romantic, and absolutely worth the stop — 5/5.

To see everything you need the whole day. Tickets need to book in advance, children below 18 are free of charge. We didn’t got a queue to enter at 17:00 on Friday ( this day it works till 21:00)

Of course it is a beautiful place with unique exhibits. The building is completely renovated, but the charm of the era is preserved.

We got a little lost in the long corridors, because the printed map does not indicate all the halls.

Be careful, there are one-way halls

A Magical Experience at the Louvre ✨️

Visiting the Louvre Museum was truly a magical experience—especially for anyone who loves history and stories. Every corner of the museum feels like stepping into a different time and place, and the atmosphere is simply breathtaking.

A helpful tip: buy your tickets online in advance! The lines can get really long, so booking ahead will save you a lot of time (and energy 😊). We were incredibly lucky—since we had our 1.5-year-old daughter with us, the friendly staff noticed and kindly let us skip the line. They were amazing with families and treated everyone with small kids with extra care and respect. It made our visit even more special.

We chose to go on Friday at 4:00 PM, hoping it would be less crowded. While it was still busy, it was definitely better than peak times on other days. So if you’re looking for a slightly calmer visit, late Friday afternoon might be a good option!

Highly recommend this unforgettable place—it’s a must-visit in Paris. 💫

A truly incredible experience getting to come to this museum. The one downside is that you’ll never be able to see everything that’s in there in one day. Be ready to walk my wife and I walked 12 miles just inside, attempting to see everything the museum had to offer. I hope one day that I’ll be able to come back and see the museum again.

It’s the Louvre Museum!

One of the largest museum exhibitions in the world (I think)

Got my ticket in advance at the official website (please be aware that there are a lot of scams so make sure it’s the official website)

I’m the kind of person to spend my time looking at the details of the art.

I’m a bit embarrassed to say that I spent a whole day there. I arrived at 12:30 and left at 8pm

Many people say that it’s a long line to get in but I disagree

The line moves pretty fast

You are allowed to bring cameras and a backpack

I saw some bring luggage and rent a locker (not sure the price on that)

But if you’re an art dork like me, prepare an entire day for this

The chaotic parts of the museum were for the Mona Lisa (as expected). There are many tour groups that are also visiting.

but if you can wait for the groups to leave the area you can peacefully enjoy the Mona Lisa and The Last Super.

Attached are some of my photos taken by my phone (took way too many on my digital and may edit the post to add some of those)

PS to all art dorks: BRING COMFORTABLE SHOES!! and take as many breaks as you need.

I hope you enjoy the Louvre as much as I did 🥹 ❤️
A treasure trove of history and art. Book tickets in advance for the morning, wear comfortable shoes

Well, we cannot see that it’s not a visited museum because it’s going through millions of people a year but in my opinion, it’s a bit overrated. You only had a few different types of paintings and art and I didn’t find any of those interesting.
Mona Lisa was very crowded but other than that their deal was OK
I recommend visiting – no
Amazing for sure. Highly recommend to get there at least an hour before your scheduled entry time. There are tons of people going through.

Huge collections of artifacts. Worth the wait, ticket can be purchase online or walk in. The queue to get inside to buy ticket is long wait. Advice to book online. You will have to go thru security. Audio guide can be rented. No restriction of bringing food and drinks, you may consume it at the lobby area only. Food and drinks inside is limited and pricy. Small bottle of mineral water around €4++. So does canned drinks. A chicken baguette is around €7. Planned your visit, manage your time so can see more. There are different wings and categories. The craziest part is visit MonaLiza. Educating and eye open experience.

Come early and be prepared to spend the day, even then you won't be able to experience everything g offered at one of the greatest museums I've ever experienced. But tickets in advance, sit down at the shop with a map and a coffee and plan out your day, it's a maze, but even if your list you're still pretty content as every corner has something amazing in it

Ticket purchased online the day before. Entered through the side entrance (Carrousel), and it literally took only 5 minutes. Helpful and friendly staff. Despite it being Saturday at noon, there were no major issues with getting closer to the artworks, looking around, or taking photos (except for the Mona Lisa), as is usually the case. In the garden be careful for the scammers. Beautiful place

I wasn't inside because I didn't see the end of the line for tickets, but I enjoyed from outside too.

Personal advice: Look out with the scammers. A lady stopped me, because she wanted some donation for deaf people, but when I give her the money (20 euro) she saw I had 50 euro currency too, and she immediately wanted to change it for me ( 1 more lady joined for this action ) . I didn't understand the situation, but I left the place immediately, because my instinct was so loud.

The lady didn't wear any charity T-shirt, I signed a very cheap printed paper, and her attitude was weird. I was kuku like hell, and 80% that money went to McDonald's or cigarettes, not for the correct place. I hope you will be smarter than me. Be careful.

Amazing museum obviously. My recommendation is to go late entry at around 7:00 pm on an evening it’s open late. Pick your top 10 must-see’s, and go and see your top 10. Anything more than that is overwhelming in my humble opinion. The late entry provided us with up close viewing of all of the touristy favorites inc. Mona Lisa, and some near-private time with some of our less-known favorites.

Tip: You can get lost rather easily, don’t waste time wondering aimlessly unless that’s your goal. There are plenty of people who work at the museum, they mostly speak English. You can show them a photo of what you’re looking for and they will point you in the right direction.

Tip: If ever you want to get back to center, look for the pyramid signs (they are everywhere) that say exit and follow the signs or again ASK someone.

We ate in the food court which was good, and very convenient, no line and no wait (and no cigarette smoke) at around 9:00pm ish. Food court closes so be mindful of your timing if you want to eat there.
We visited the Louvre Museum in July 2025—truly an unforgettable experience, and the photos say it all.

One of the world's best museums, and for good reason. So much beautiful stuff to see here, but make sure you buy tickets in advance and try to avoid weekends and busy tourist months. There aren't a ton of spaces to sit, even in the food areas and of course near the most famous pieces of art, so plan ahead and take advantage of a bench when you see it. Best to attend with good shoes and a bottle of water. And leave time after your visit to enjoy a walk in the Jardin des Tuileries; it's just across the road and worth a nice slow stroll.

This place could impress just by size alone, it’s so huge. And to think it is full of art 😱. We saw almost all of the expositions that were open that day in about 4-5 hours without any type of guide, but it definitely deserves more time and attention. Saw a lot of famous pieces and was again in awe at the paintings and sculptures. The palace is sooo big, we ended up asking for directions a few times 😅 but this was mainly because some parts were closed due to renovation and we had to turn back instead of just continuing the tour. We visited on a friday noon and it was quite crowded inside, but at least the lane moved very fast at the entrance. The drinks were a bit overpriced though, definitely bring your own bottle of water.

A medieval fortress, later a royal palace, now a public museum after the French Revolution in 1793.

All the buildings and the river look amazing at nighttime.

An unforgettable masterpiece of a museum

Visiting the Louvre was truly one of the highlights of my time in Paris. From the moment you step beneath the iconic glass pyramid, you feel the weight of history, art, and human creativity all around you. The museum itself is breathtaking — a magnificent former palace that is as impressive as the treasures it holds.
The collection is extraordinary in both scale and quality. Seeing world-famous works like the Mona Lisa, the Venus de Milo, and the Winged Victory of Samothrace in person was unforgettable, but what surprised me most was discovering incredible pieces in quieter galleries throughout the museum. Every room tells a story, spanning thousands of years and countless cultures.
Despite its size, the Louvre is well organized, and the staff were helpful and welcoming. I appreciated the clear signage and the many places to pause, reflect, and simply take in the beauty around me. It’s impossible to see everything in one visit, which only makes me want to return.
The Louvre is not just a museum — it’s a journey through art, history, and civilization. Whether you’re an art expert or a first-time visitor, it’s an inspiring, awe-inspiring experience that lives up to its legendary reputation. I would recommend it to anyone visiting Paris without hesitation.
Must see if in Paris. All exhibits beautiful. We came for the Mona Lisa but it has so much to offer.

I visited the Louvre Museum at night and, although I didn’t take the tour inside, the experience was amazing. The exterior architecture is stunning, especially when lit up at night. Walking around the area feels magical, the pyramid, the courtyards, and the historical atmosphere make it absolutely worth visiting, even just from the outside. An iconic place that radiates art and history just by being near it.
The Louvre Museum is so AMAZING! It is wonderful to walk through and see all the history! My favorite thing to see was Da Vinci's Mona Lisa! That piece of art is truly captivating and beautiful.

The Louvre is an amazing place to soak up culture. It is truly huge, but we managed to see everything we were interested in within about 3–4 hours. We didn’t spend too long in front of each exhibit, which made it possible. I highly recommend visiting the Napoleon III Apartments, which are located in the Richelieu Wing on the first floor. They are stunning and give you a glimpse into the luxury of that era.

I have to take off one star because of an unpleasant encounter with one staff member.

Luckily, the other staff members were very kind and helpful, and thanks to them we were still able to enjoy many beautiful things in this wonderful place of world culture and history.

Wonderful place, I loved the experience!
Me and my group spent more less 3 hours inside the museum to see the principal arts.
best to visit through guided tour.  so much eye candy and history.  no doubt, a must see

The Louvre Museum is absolutely breathtaking! Make sure to set aside a full day to really explore and enjoy everything it has to offer. If you book your tickets in advance with a timed entry, you’ll avoid long lines and save a lot of time. Once inside, don’t miss the chance to try France’s famous pastries at the cafes near the entrance, they’re truly delicious and the perfect complement to an amazing day at the museum.

Buy tickets in advance, and prepare yourself for being with a lot of people (long queues to the bathroom are to be expected). It's enormous, so I prepared the visit in advance, so I could visit the things I wanted to see, and if I wasn't too tired, I'd just check other things. There isn't a lot of signs or indications, and the maps that are provided by the Museum are not really good, but you can always politely ask to the museum's staff, and they will always guide you and they are super nice and helpful.

It was a short time in Paris so I can't book the ticket in advance. And the day I come to the museum close( on Tuesday). You can book the tickets 🎟 from the website.
But I had a chance to visit around, it was a very big place. If you want to see all you need to take more days for it.

You have to book before you come. If not, you'll have to wait a few hours to buy a ticket. It was a famous place and I guess it was a main museum in Paris.
I was going down to get some relax and play some game 🎮 down there. That was a fun and great experience.
I might come back to visit the Louvre museum next time.
Highly recommended when you visit Paris

I recommend entering the Louvre from the Carrousel side entrance. If you go through the Pyramid, you may waste time in a long line under the sun. Internet is very weak here, but the museum is truly incredible. I strongly recommend visiting—it is one of the greatest experiences in the world.

Buy tickets in advance, follow the directions of entry (on the ticket you will have an entrance option).
Busy but doable in March, weekday.
With regards to the art: it’s cool but not all tooms were available during my visit. Very diverse collections

There's no need to explain. It's a must-see, a place you should dedicate a full day to. If you don't bring children, you'll be comfortable and spend the day there. The place is packed with unique works of art.

The Louvre is absolutely breathtaking, every gallery feels like stepping into a different world, and there’s simply too much beauty to take in during just one visit. One day is never enough to truly appreciate it all. I’m already looking forward to coming back and discovering even more next time!

Absolutely incredible. Make sure you give yourself enough time to visit! Cute little spot for sandwiches inside. Long line to see the Mona Lisa. Not recommended for young children. And yes, go to the gift shop.
Expansive, amazing, cant been taken in in one day.  You MUST  buy tickets in advance, the number of people can be overwhelming

Mid week one day city trip to Paris
Absolutely amazing experience! Purchasing tickets online was incredibly easy, and booking a time slot meant there was no queuing at all, which made everything stress-free. The sheer size of the place is truly impressive. Everything was spotless, and I found the Purusans to be very polite and welcoming. I would suggest purchasing earphones or hiring a guide to help explain the artifacts. But overall, it was a fantastic visit!

Highlights: The Louvre can get busy, with lots of people trying to take photos, but it wasn’t long before your turn. The museum houses thousands of masterpieces, but some of its most famous works include the Mona Lisa by Leonardo da Vinci, Venus de Milo (an Ancient Greek sculpture), Winged Victory of Samothrace (a Hellenistic sculpture), Liberty Leading the People by Eugène Delacroix, and The Raft of the Medusa by Théodore Géricault.
A tour was nice to see the main highlights. It’s massive, so if you have no guide, plan ahead your route and must sees.

An iconic treasure trove of art and history! Spread over a vast area, the Louvre could take a week to fully explore if you want to see it all properly. A must-visit in Paris for art and culture lovers.

My second time at this museum. We bought tickets online for about 22eu each and stood in line for our time slot. It is recommended to buy the tickets before to skip the big queue.
It’s impossible to cover all the parts of the museum in few hours. Try to reserve a whole day to explore the museum in leisure.
A lifetime of viewing . Everyone talks of whats inside the building. The entire structure is engineering art.Built on a castle. Stunning achievement.

Incredible art gallery. The scale of the place is awe inspiring, you could easily spend a week here and still not see everything. The guided tour was excellent to give an overview of the Louvre and cover some of the main sights
Amazing museum experience. The artworks of the late artists were astonishingly amazing, they were so talented.

The buildings are just so immense and big with perfect architectural design and maintenance🤩.I had a beautiful experience there. The museum is just so beautiful with benches for people to sit and enjoy 😉 the moment ❤️ and to take some snacks 😋 if you went with some sandwiches, nuts 😋 water, juice (little things like that) you'll be pleased 😌joined with a good weather I guarantee 💯
Amazing experience! Definitely pay extra to have a private tour. Our guide said it would take 2 weeks to see everything for 12 seconds! The museum is enormous and worth every trip you make to it!

We had a great time exploring the Louvre Museum!
There is so much to see and learn as you go through. Obviously, it is very popular, so I would suggest getting your tickets in advance and get there early enough, so you have plenty of time to see everything.
The ticket prices are pretty reasonable, considering all that there is to explore.
The Louvre is very well maintained and emaculate from start to finish. all of the staff and guides we encountered were professional, couteous and very knowledgeable.
Considering that 8 to 9 million visitors come every year, we did not feel overcrowded at any point. the busiest area was probably viewing the Mona Lisa and even that was not too crowded.

Exploring The Louvre was definitely one of my favorite things to do this vacation, and I'm not much of an art person. My personal favorites were the Crown and collection of beautiful jewels/jewelry from Dutch Royalty and of course the infamous Mona Lisa!

Highly Recommend a visit!

Beautifully grand. De Louvre has multiple floors and separate “wings” that display seperate genres of artwork. You expect the artwork to be amazing, but the building itself was magnificent! You must purchase tickets in advance because they will be sold out, but if you have tickets there is little to no wait to enter. For easier navigation proceed to the information desk and acquire a map (available in multiple languages) and plan a route accordingly. Once you have visited one wing, proceed back to the center starting area to enter a seperate wing. Bring comfortable shoes as you will spend hours here. #1 museum in the world for a reason.

Visited the Louvre on a Friday, then again the following Sunday. This place is awesome. My son and I loved it. As you would expect, it's a bit crowded around the Mona Lisa, but once you get away from that area of the museum it's not too bad at all. There is something here for everyone. Btw... I visited the Mona Lisa again on Sunday afternoon around 5:15pm right before closing time and there was hardly anyone around. Definitely a good time to see it if you can wait.
A very beautiful and artful place , it’s very big and cannot be completed very soon, you require 2 days atleast to see the whole museum at full speed , every room is unique and great

Lots to see inside and in the gardens.
Very busy except queues and book days if not weeks in advance. Duration of the visit most of the day.

Soooo much detail everywhere. You really can’t go through everything in a single visit, so it’s a good idea to choose a route or section you want to focus on beforehand. The staff were helpful and the information provided was useful. Also, make sure your cameras and phones are fully charged before you arrive because there is a lot worth photographing. If possible, save an entire day just for visiting this place so you can enjoy it without rushing.
Largest art museum in the world. Overwhelming...too much, too many people...but you must see it!

Its a must a do while in Paris. I got as close as you can while taking a photo of the Mona Lisa...so pretty happy with it. The rest of the Lourve was great, I was pretty much done though by about 2.5 has into looking around. Best best like most things book in as early as you can 9am and have a plan before going in and check off all the stuff you want to see first  then look at the rest after. What happens is they have half hourly entries so by midday the place is packed and difficult to see the good stuff.

An absolute bucket list of a museum. I personally feel you need 5-7 days to breathe in every corner and nook of this superior establishment with awe-inspiring, priceless art. They also utilize the Nintendo 3DS for mapping and audio. Such genius.

It was overwhelming. If you stop at each exhibit it will take you 4 years to see. We soured for three hours.
Only critical comment is the condition of the toilets.. filthy!

Absolutely superb, a museum at its best, countless works of art, superb architecture. I recommend entering through the metro under the pyramid and buying tickets in advance so you don't have to worry about waiting too long. At this entrance there are maps in several languages ​​and lockers to leave your things. Wherever you turn your head you will find something to like. Obviously all the attention is on the room with the Mona Lisa but there are many paintings that are worth seeing besides it. It is absolutely huge! I recommend giving a day exclusively to this museum in the morning to try to see all the floors and sections! I highly recommend this museum! It is absolutely necessary to see it if you arrive in Paris! If you are under 26 years old, access is free!

Lots of people but still feel amazing. Get the ticket at 15:30 with only 2.5 hours in museum is never enough. Amazing work from artists and the beautiful designs are breathtaking.

For lesser people, recommend to go on weekday or earlier to reserve enough time for the visit

The Louvre Museum in Paris is an absolute must-visit! This iconic museum is not only home to some of the world’s most famous artworks, like the Mona Lisa and the Venus de Milo, but it’s also set in a breathtaking historic palace that adds to the overall experience.

The museum itself is a vibrant hub of culture and creativity, with countless exhibits showcasing art from various periods and regions. The layout is easy to navigate, and every corner offers something new and exciting to discover.

Beyond the art, the surrounding area of Paris is alive with energy, from delightful cafés and bakeries to charming shops and street performances. You can easily spend days exploring the vibrant neighborhoods nearby, soaking in the atmosphere.

The fun and vibrancy of both the Louvre and Paris make for an unforgettable experience that combines history, culture, and the joy of exploring a beautiful city. It’s definitely a highlight for any traveler!

The Louvre is absolutely massive and breathtaking—definitely a must-visit in Paris. The art, history, and architecture are incredible, but it does get very crowded, so be ready for long lines and lots of walking. Highly recommend booking tickets in advance and giving yourself at least half a day, if not more. Seeing the Mona Lisa was cool, but the museum has so much more to offer beyond that. An unforgettable experience

Due to limited time and heavy rain, I could only explore the Louvre from the outside — but even that took a while, with its stunning details and incredible architecture. The historic presence is overwhelming. If you plan to visit inside, make sure to book tickets in advance.
Magnificent collection. I will need at least few visits to embrace it fully.

It's a great place for art and history lovers. But about 80% of people come here only for Mona Lisa. But the museum is so huge, I don't think 1 whole day is enough to see everything they have to offer.
Very crowded. I recommend you buy tickets in advance. It is the largest art museum in the world! If you want to go to Eiffel Tower please do not take the 3 wheeled rickshaw. They can cheat you.

Beautiful and well worth it. Buy your online tickets in advance. The only thing I find unfair is that you're in line to take a look at the Monalisa and they let people with priority pass to look at the pianting upclose while you can't get an inch close. They just stay there too long blocking your view and it's already time to exit the line. There's no line per se but a mass of people gathered around pushing you from all directions. Maybe the museum needs to work on this. The audio guides are incomplete, too short and from numbers 400 (important artwork) onwards are exclusively for kids when I too needed to listen. Even if you have bought your online ticket, you still need to wait in a very long line to get in and another line for the audio guides. A part from this all's good.

Not as great as I had hoped.  VERY busy.  Likely the busiest place we visited in Paris.  Hard to find your way around.  Would get a guide next time.  The audio addon was not that great.  Hard to use.  We missed so much stuff due to the size and the crowds. Fyi, The Mona Lisa took about 15 mins to get to the front of the line. Overall very good but not great.  We enjoyed Versailles more.

I’m not a huge art fan myself, but my teenage daughter is, and going to the Louvre museum was a must during our visit to Paris. I’m so glad she made this a must stop, I had zero idea the Louvre buildings were formerly a palace and was then a tremendously interesting backdrop for the art collections. I would have been super pleased just walking the palace buildings without all the art! There was also a really great section of the museum that focused on the history of the Louvre itself, which I absolutely loved as it showed how and when it was built. The French monarchy were incredible builders, such unbelievable scale, details throughout everything they built, just amazing.

The crowds are insane, and hard to imagine the chaos happens here everyday (nearly), but u guess it does. The global interest in this museum is impressive to experience, and seeing it firsthand, it honestly deserves the hype and tremendous reputation and fame it possesses.

Tickets in advance are probably a must, we arrived about 30 minutes after our reserved time which turned out to be a very good thing as we were able to avoid a long line and basically just walk in.

Only regret was that we didn’t know until visiting that a lot of art we expected to see isn’t here, basically anything after 1850 is at a different museum, so Monet, Van Gogh, and others are at this other one. We didn’t know this and didn’t have time to do another day at the museum, so missed these completely. Oh well, good excuse to visit Paris again!

Omg a great museum, the bad thing is the long line to get in,about 40 minutes. The good thing there is art wherever you see, I had the dream to see the monalisa live and direct and I did it! However it was crowded especially where the monalisa is located. You have to make a line for over 15 minutes just to see her.

This was a fun experience. There are a TON of people though, so if you're easily claustrophobic or struggle with crowds, this is NOT the place for you. Also there was a guy at the ticket line that was not helpful at all and a bit rude, so make sure you know what you're doing and where you're going and what time your tickets are ahead of time.

Mind-blowing! You could spend weeks in here without seeing the same thing twice. So many treasures! Be prepared if you are anxious or claustrophobic - the spaces are big and airy, but the layout is labrynthine and exiting can take a while, so worth planning for.

The Louvre Museum is a beautiful place and definitely a must-visit if you're in Paris. It’s one of those spots where you can actually feel the history around you. The collection inside is massive... you’ll see everything from world-famous paintings to ancient jewellery, clothes, sculptures, pottery, and a lot more. It’s like walking through a timeline of human creativity.

One thing to keep in mind...the place is huge. You’ll end up walking a lot, so wear comfortable shoes and give yourself enough time. It’s not something you can rush through in an hour.

Of course, the Mona Lisa is the star attraction, and it’s worth seeing just for the experience. But don’t stop there...there are so many other beautiful paintings and exhibits that are equally impressive. I’d also recommend checking out the jewellery section... some of the pieces there are just mind-blowing in detail and design.

If you're someone who enjoys art, history, or just wants to see something unique, you won’t be disappointed. The whole vibe of the place is amazing, and it really sticks with you. Just take your time and enjoy it.. it’s one of the highlights of Paris.

Note:
The museum entry is free on every months first Friday...Enjoy!

Absolutely nothing like it in the world! Expect very long lines. We have tickets for 10:30 AM in the morning and it took approximately 40 minutes to enter.

Rain and cold so definitely check the weather and dress appropriately. Take extra precautions and be ready for miles of walking. I really mean miles walking.

An incredible museum and truly a must-see in Paris. The building itself is beautiful and full of history. It’s huge, so you can’t see everything in one day, but that’s part of the charm. The Mona Lisa is nice to see (though smaller and crowded), but I enjoyed the quieter sections even more. Amazing collection and atmosphere overall.

The Louvre Museum is absolutely breathtaking. From the iconic glass pyramid to the endless galleries filled with masterpieces like the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo, every moment inside feels special. The collection is incredibly vast, covering art and history from all over the world. It’s impossible to see everything in one visit which just makes you want to come back again. A must-see for anyone visiting Paris!

Lots of art, you can literally walk for hours and keep finding new stuff. The security staff needs to keep the lines organized to see the Mona Lisa. The food at the café was disappointing, better save that money and go to the bakery right next to the museum.

The Louvre is honestly one of the most amazing places I’ve ever visited! It’s huge — you could spend the whole day there and still not see everything. The artworks are incredible, from the Mona Lisa to all the ancient sculptures and paintings. The building itself is stunning too, especially the glass pyramid. I went there while studying abroad, and it honestly felt surreal seeing all those masterpieces in real life. Totally worth visiting — such a beautiful and inspiring place!

Incredible

You can spend days here

39000 art pieces displayed

Legend has it 450000 total collection
Spectacular, incredible, and the grounds are equally beautiful! You can't see it all but you do see is breathtaking

One of the best museums I've ever seen.
Only be prepared to walk A LOT because the things are far from eachother. It's a huge museum, you need more than a full day to see it all; in fact, I segguest you pick the things you wanna see the most.

A must visit, although bring your patience as the crowds are enormous near the most famous pieces. Annoying when some are doing photo shoots of at least 100 selfies with fish lips instead of admiring the art. As you get into the other galleries, there is more space to find what catches your interest and stay longer.

An unforgettable experience! It’s the biggest and one of the most visited museums in the world.
The Louvre is truly a treasure trove of world history and art — from the Mona Lisa’s mysterious smile to the grand halls lined with masterpieces. Although the lines are long, it’s absolutely worth the wait. Give yourself plenty of time — you’ll want to get lost in its endless beauty!

Amazing experience that’s special
Even spending one day is not enough to cover 1/4 of the exhibits. You’d have to do it over multiple trips.
Getting a guide would let you jump the queue
Costs more, but saves you hours.

All I will say is this place is MASSİVE. Please Before visiting, plan your whole day to spend your time at Louvre. There were amazing sculptures and paintings all around it. It is mainly seperated into 3 parts. Please find a brochure and also a reminder all parts are connected with each other at each level so before going up one level go around the whole floor.

Absolutely stunning museum... my family and I visited for over 4 hours and we didn't even scratch the surface... but we saw the Mona Lisa, maddona on the rocks, Venus de milo and the twin pyramids.... saw some stunning art works and sculptures....

We are Greeks,and the moment we saw this master piece,was breath taking.
But we are also parents...and we didn't see as much as we want to see.
So it will be a next time for us 😉🤞

A Must-Visit in Paris
I’m not usually a big fan of museums, but the Louvre completely blew me away. We only spent a short time there since it wasn’t part of our original plan—but wow, one day isn’t nearly enough. The collection inside is incredible, and seeing the Mona Lisa in person is surreal. The way the crowd gathers around it shows just how iconic it is.

From the outside, the museum is a stunning mix of old and modern architecture—you’d never guess how massive it is until you’re inside. It’s truly one of those places that leaves you speechless. Next time we’re in Paris, we’re definitely spending the whole day there.

Great place but i do not reccomend buying ticket before... Maybe in high season its a good idea but i was in may and people who do not have ticket was faster than people with ticket... You are waiting in long line... After you get inside you can take a map but its soo much to see and the most popular paints or attraction are very busy so you need to be patient and have a good knowledge of map... I was lost do many times but there are workers who always help you :) what is great! A lot of walking...
This is a must visit. So many beautiful art inside. Even the exterior is aesthetically beautiful and majestic

25 years since my previous visit. Seems even busier now. Suggest pre-booking tickets and I would look to get a guided tour. We relied on my limited knowledge of the works 😅.
I spent the next day at the outside of the Louvre on Sunrise and had the place to myself, would suggest if the opportunity arises to head down early in the morning or later at night. We did both and it was magic.

Get your tickets early and in advance… this bloody place is heaving.

Also the Mona Lisa is everything it’s hyped up to be. A painting. Which, you can’t get all that near to…

Truly beautiful. Unless you plan on spending the entire day its impossible to see all the pieces. There are restaurants on site so you can grab food in between. There wasnt much of à lineup and I went through security without a hassle. There are Louvre personnels everywhere so you can ask questions whenever you feel lost

A visit to the Louvre is a must. The architecture is amazing. The art offers something for everyone. Avoid crowds with an evening visit. Buying tickets in advance is recommended. If you're hungry, there is food. We tried a cafe. Pastries looked wonderful. Coffee too. The quality was poor. This didn't diminish our visit. Every minute in the museum was a memorable experience.

You need 2 full weeks to see everything in this amazing place. We had jist under 3 hours.

We picked our "must sees" and luckily saw them all. This is a place I've wanted to see my whole life. It did not disappoint. I can't wait to go back.

I loved the Louvre it was great but the only thing that let me down was that the royal crown section was closed but despite that I loved the louvre it had amazing pieces and when my feet were tired there were even little benches.
Visiting the Louvre was phenomenal. The Mona Lisa is a bigger than life painting however seeing the actual size of the Mona Lisa was surreal.

The staff was unfriendly and didn’t let me go for free even though i’m an european citizen under 25 they said my document wasn’t enough to prove it but im literally a resident here that really sucked but it’s worth paying 22 euros for the louvre definitely one of the best museums in the world

A truly iconic establishment housing a fantastic collection of art.
The various buildings are without doubt very stylish and a credit to the French nation and Architecture and Culture.
There are several very large plazas and gardens which are all well designed and maintained.
Definitely a must visit when you are in Paris.
You should spend the whole day to enjoy vast buildings, it's contents and the site as a whole.
Beautiful culture beautiful people and staff. Lines are ridiculous but worth the wait. Phone died after 2nd photo inside. Welp

This museum is said to be the world's largest and it is huge. I visited with a tour although our group was in my opinion too large at 24 people. The tour guide knows the highlights and moves you through them efficiently as navigating on your own is difficult at best as there are many  many rooms that create a maze like effect.

Time stamped for 6pm entry on a late museum day.We entered at 5:30! Stayed until closing, at 9,  on Wednesday night. The museum stays open until 9PM on Wednesdays and Fridays. Highly recommend. We saw the highlights we wanted, plus extras and enjoyed a lighter crowd i. All areas except the Mona Lisa, but we're invthe front of the group in under 2 minutes. It was wonderful!

We weren't able to go inside, but judging from only the sizes of the building, one day is not enough. We only took a little peak of the statue park, close to the Pyramid. But the building itself is also truly amazing! A masterpiece of architecture if you ask me. If you don't go inside at least a walk around the museum is a must if you are in Paris, we did that!

What can you say…..biggest museum in the world. We used guide that helped with quicker access and insightful commentary for a 2 hour tour that helps you feel like you really experienced the important things to see.

I’d recommend not trying to see it all….take your time and enjoy what you do see. Break for lunch or a drink. Enjoy more. And then go look for a French meal. Trying to see it all just creates a blur of everything.

Oh. And the Mona Lisa is there as well. No need to wait in the huge group. To save a half hour just walk past the group to the next room and see it very close but just off to the side a little.

Bon chance!
Utmost beautiful place to visit. Make sure you have enough time to explore everything. And take breaks. Its a marathon, not a sprint.

This is without a doubt the best museum I've ever been to. I can't express how amazing it is. Floor to ceiling, wall to wall, absolutely packed to the brim with the finest art you'll ever have the privilege to see. I was overwhelmed by the size and magnitude of this place.

Do yourself a favor by grabbing a map and don't be in a hurry. You could easily spend all day inside. And definitely wear comfy shoes! There's tons of ground to cover and lots of stairs.

Buy tickets in advance!

We bought tickets for 10am entry and the crowds weren't too bad. By the time we left, around 2pm, the place was packed. O.o

I highly recommend buying tickets in advance. Even with pre-purchased tickets, be prepared to wait in line for about an hour before entering. The place is absolutely massive – you would need several days to see everything. One full day is enough to see the main highlights, but it requires a lot of walking and energy.

Security checks bags and backpacks at the entrance, but you are allowed to bring snacks and drinks with you. There's also a café inside, although we decided to skip it after waiting around 40 minutes in line – it might be worth considering adding more food options on-site.

If you want to see the Mona Lisa, head straight there. We visited outside of peak season, and the line was still huge. Once you manage to get close, you’ll have maybe a minute to admire the painting and take a photo. If you're into the truly great masterpieces, you'll find fewer crowds in other areas – and they are absolutely worth your time.

In my opinion, this is the best museum in the world. I could honestly spend years there, haha. As someone who loves history, the highlight for me was seeing the Code of Hammurabi. I was surprised it's not more crowded – it's an incredible piece of human history.

Highly recommended, and despite the queues, I will definitely come back. I’m curious to discover more hidden gems that will make me fall even more in love with this museum.

An exceptionally rich museum located in a historic palace boasting eight hundred years of history and beautiful architecture.

Here are some helpful tips for your visit (maximum 5 hours):
▪️ Booking your visit online is essential;
▪️ Choose a relatively small area of the museum to visit based on your artistic interests and stay within that space during your visit (the museum map is available online);
▪️ The Mona Lisa is overrated!!! Explore the Grande Galerie instead;
▪️ As an alternative to viewing the artworks, I recommend exploring the palace and admiring its splendid architecture.

Loved it, it is really amazing 😍 we bought the tickets in advance and spent only 30 minutes in a line as it was supposed. We did not have much time but three hours were enough to see what i wanted especially the Louvre Couture ❤️ audio guide appeared quite complicated 🤣 but the security people were very friendly and helpful 💖
If you are going to visit Louvre do not plan anything else on that day simply enjoy with a nice walk in the park or surrounding streets and try local cafe or restaurant...

We visited the Louvre on a Sunday and had an incredible experience! We bought our tickets in advance and only waited about 15 minutes before entering. Once you’re inside, it’s absolutely breathtaking, room after room of stunning paintings, sculptures, and world-famous works of art.

Even though it was quite crowded, it didn’t take away from the experience. The museum is beautifully organized, and every turn reveals something amazing. Definitely buy your tickets ahead of time and use one of the less popular entrances as the guides recommend, it saves so much time and makes your visit even more enjoyable. A must-see in Paris!

One of the biggest museums in the world. The history behind the building itself is interesting as much as the art inside it. There are thousands of pieces on display and there's noway anyone can manage to see everything in one day and it can be overwhelming. So my recommendation is to focus on the main interests and let the museum guide you for the rest of the day.
Too busy and chaotic but obviously architecture and art works are 😻 amazing! Advice: book a ticket in advance and have your own snacks or water as its so expensive there and in the area

I was so surreal to be at one of the amazing tourist places I only saw in movies like Angles and Demons... Kudos to the French for preserving the heritage...

Would like to see the tomb of Mary next time, though... 😆
Wow, amazing place. We spent at least 5-6 hours and it was barely minimum to visit most of stuff.

Such a beautiful place! The views are amazing at any angle! The courtyard, the garden and surrounding buildings are absolutely breathtaking. Spend some time, if you're going into the museum I would make a day of it as there is so much to see inside! The garden in the front also boasts seating and little shacks of food to eat as well! So much to see and do!
The Louve is an overwhelming place.  So much to see.  I'd recommend people hire a personal guide to put all the fabulous art in perspective.   Definitely not an afternoons task
I think by far one of the best museums in the world! Impressive and E N O R M O U S  🫨

The museum is huge and truly impressive, with so much history and art to explore. Since it is very big, the best approach is to plan ahead and focus on specific monuments or artworks you really want to see. Definitely worth visiting!
Incredible! One visit isn’t enough though. I’m glad I bought tickets in advance.
This museum is INCREDIBLE. Be prepared to walk and be amazed with all of the history and art.

It was a really cool experience overall. The Mona Lisa was way way over crowded at Noon on a Wednesday. I recommend going around 2:00pm to start the museum. The crowds really die down. But it was super cool to see Napoleon's apartment, royal jewelry collection, and the couture designer fashion pieces.

Great museum with some of the greatest works of art in the history he word!  It does get crowded, so check the best times to go.  It was very crowded (5-6 rows deep) to see the Mona Lisa, but you can work your way to the front for pictures.
The most impressive museum I have ever been. I would say you should leave a whole day for it!
A rich museum experience + free student tickets = a truly wonderful experience!

Unforgettable experience. Book the 9:00 am slot, get there at 8:30, and when they open walk straight to the Mona Lisa. If you do this, you’ll walk right up to it and avoid a long and crowded wait later in the day. The tour guides are very knowledgeable and will enrich the experience.

However, you only get two scans with your pass. If you enter at 9:00 am and then have a tour, you will have to leave the ticketed area to join the tour group. When you reenter you’ll use your second pass. So be prepared to either stay after your tour or book second day.
It was worth it to see the Mona Lisa, but alas, I wasn't much interested in the other exhibits. Taking nothing away from the Museum though. It's a beautiful place and absolutely enormous

When I lived in Paris, I had an annual pass. It was so worth it. Special entrance. Rarely a line. No reservations. Best 80€ to spend. Did it again this trip, so I could come and go for short trips. Did the late hours on Wednesday. The couture exhibits are exquisite!

The Louvre is always a good idea.
I booked my ticket a month in advance, and maybe that’s why everything felt so seamless—even the rain didn’t matter. I entered through a quieter side entrance, skipping the chaos at the main gate, and for a moment, it felt like the museum opened just for me.

Yes, it was crowded—tourists everywhere—and no, I didn’t see the Mona Lisa up close. I stood about 20 feet back, zoomed in with my camera, and took it in from afar. Too. Many. Tourists. !!!!! And still… it was enough.

Because the real magic of the Louvre is not in one painting—it’s in the endless corridors of history, light, shadow, and silence between brushstrokes. It’s the quiet awe that settles in your chest as you move from sculpture to canvas, room to room, century to century.

I spent four hours inside and could have stayed four more. The Louvre doesn’t just house art—it holds time, memory, and wonder. It’s not just a museum; it’s a feeling. One that lingers.

The Louvre is an obvious choice when you visit Paris.  My suggestion for the best time without tons of people is to visit in the off season, like February.  It's cold and sometimes dreary outside but not inside!  Get there early so you can spend all day there.  Take your time to browse.  Stop and have lunch at Angelina's to recharge.  Do your research before you go.  What do you want to see and where EXACTLY is it.  The provided map is ok but there are so many amazing things to see it helps if you plan.  Or, don't.  Just wander and get lost.  If you happen to go during the couture exhibition go!!  It's amazing!
Unmatched beautiful art and architecture.       An absolute must see when in Paris.   Sad that the fountains were not turned on due to protests

Walking into the Louvre felt like stepping into a living boardroom of human history — every corridor a department, every gallery a strategy deck crafted across centuries. Even in the mid-morning weekday flow, the energy was calm, focused, and surprisingly efficient. I entered through the Richelieu Wing, and within minutes the noise of the city dissolved into marble floors, soft footsteps, and sculptures that felt almost alive.

What struck me wasn’t only the art, but the sense of momentum inside the building — artists sketching in corners, sunlight moving across stone, visitors whispering like they’re afraid to disturb the past. Standing in front of Delacroix’s dramatic canvases and the grand marble statues, I felt the same thing I feel when running high-impact service at a restaurant: precision, emotion, and discipline all living together.

If you love storytelling, this museum is a masterclass. If you love beauty, it delivers without negotiation.
My advice:
• Visit on a weekday—the flow is smoother, and you can breathe with the art.
• Have a plan but allow detours—the unexpected rooms became my favorite chapters.
• Look for the natural light—the Louvre is at its best when sunlight hits the textures of the sculptures.

This isn’t just a museum. It’s a reminder that human creativity outlives everything.
Nice museum. Worth visiting. Monalisa and many more painting to see and also to visualize how the people lived in ancient time

Louvre …amazing, wonderful, fascinating. A program that is not to be missed if you are in Paris. If you can only choose one museum, this should be it.
You will have to come back several times, because it is impossible to visit in one day. From 13:00 to 20:00 we were able to visit about half of the museum actively. You can book tickets online.
I would like to highlight that the staff is 10/10, EXTREMELY attentive. We arrived with a disabled person, they helped us with full attention and assistance throughout. You can easily move around the entire museum with a wheelchair, everything is clearly signposted, you can easily find your way around. If you come by car, you can easily park under the Louvre, which unfortunately is not cheap, but very practical. The gift shops are also expensive, but you can buy souvenirs that you cannot find anywhere else.

Absolutely fantastic experience! Please know that it will take you much longer than you think, and you still won’t see everything you wanted to. The line for the Mona Lisa is long, yes, which struck me as very funny given that the hall adjoining it has several other DaVinci paintings that go virtually ignored in comparison - and they are, obviously, fantastic. There are other works that typically don’t get spoken of as much too - icons, ancient near eastern, etc. (Never mind the ceilings!) Utterly extraordinary!

Extraordinary museum with the iconic glass pyramid and Mona Lisa which is getting crazier to see with all of the reels and selfies. If you can book Cafe marly to drink in the whole view. It was Paris fashion so lots of designers and models there. Food and service amazing. Also book a time slot to enter the Louvre. All was so easy.

Spent the morning and early afternoon at the Louvre and completely fell in love with the Richelieu Wing on the first floor. I didn’t even make it to the other wings (had a plane to catch), but honestly? No regrets. The couture section in particular was everything—such a beautiful blend of fashion, craftsmanship, and Parisian history. And Napoleon’s apartment? Pure opulence. Every detail felt like a love letter to beauty and power. I left feeling inspired, grounded, and a little awestruck. If you’re short on time, Richelieu is more than worth the visit. Can’t wait to go back to see the rest of it.

The Louvre Museum is massive and filled with incredible artwork from all over the world. We saw countless masterpieces, from ancient sculptures to iconic paintings. Depending on your personal taste, some sections might feel overwhelming or less engaging, but the historical and cultural depth is impressive. It’s a journey through time and civilizations, offering something for everyone. A must-see for art and history lovers!
Awesome experience... One day not enough to thoroughly enjoy the works... Apart from all else, Seeing the Monalisa in reality was something!

This is, of course, a must see in Paris. As with many other reviews, get there early. If possible, get there 30 minutes before it opens. Have a plan if you have certain things you want to see and do it as soon as you get inside. There is a huge difference if you wait to see the Mona Lisa or any other major piece of art. Once you have seen those on your list, take your time and look at all of the other great pieces in there. If you truly want to see a lot of it you will have to spend more than one day. Buy your tickets as far in advance as you can to get the time and date you want.

The Louvre is a must-see when visiting Paris. It’s massive, with so much to explore—you could spend the whole day there and still not see everything! The collection is incredible, from ancient artifacts to world-famous masterpieces like the Mona Lisa.

It does get really crowded, especially around popular spots like the Mona Lisa, so I recommend booking your ticket in advance to skip the long lines. Despite the crowds, it’s absolutely worth the visit. An unforgettable experience for art and history lovers!

Visiting the Louvre Museum was an incredible experience and truly a highlight of the trip. The museum is giant and filled with world-famous artworks, including the iconic Mona Lisa. Every section feels like stepping into a different era of history and culture.

I visited in October, which was a good time—still busy, but more manageable than peak season. The glass pyramid entrance adds a modern touch to the historic setting, and the overall organization is quite good considering the size.

It’s easy to spend hours here, so planning ahead is important. Overall, it’s a must-visit for anyone interested in art, history, or simply experiencing one of the world’s greatest museums.

A Must-Visit in Paris!
The Louvre Museum is an absolute must-visit when in Paris. It’s truly worth spending a full day exploring — every corner offers something fascinating. The collection is incredibly rich, featuring art from all around the world. The Napoleon Apartments are stunning, beautifully preserved, and shine with royal elegance. Of course, seeing the Mona Lisa in person was unforgettable, but there’s so much more to discover beyond it. I especially enjoyed reading the stories behind each piece — it really brings the art to life. A wonderful experience overall!
I highly recommend buying tickets online in advance — it saves a lot of time.
Enjoy

The Louvre is overwhelming in the best way. We went in knowing we couldn’t see it all, so we focused on a few highlights—and still felt like we were walking through centuries of brilliance.

Highly recommend booking tickets in advance and going early to beat the biggest crowds. Give yourself time to take breaks—the courtyard and café areas are great for that. A must for anyone visiting Paris, even if museums aren’t usually your thing.

Not much else to say other than DON'T TOUCH ANYTHING! The amount of people Isaw putting their oily, disgusting fingers all over tapestry and frames was mind-blowing. There's a separation for a reason. This is why we can'thave nice things. Staff was helpful.

Food within the canteen is expensive so be warned and seats are classed as hostile environment i.e. not designed to be comfortable for long periods of time. The experience was generally positive, with numerous exhibits available for viewing and exploration. However, several inaccuracies were noted in the presented information, particularly regarding historical facts. For instance, certain maps displayed incorrect place names, which can be verified through historical evidence. A specific example includes the mislabeling of the Persian Gulf as simply "Golf." Given the historical context, the original and accurate designation of "Persian Gulf" should be maintained, and the museum should adhere to established historical documentation.

Furthermore, the Louvre Museum serves as a significant international tourist destination, attracting millions of visitors globally. To enhance the visitor experience, particularly for the substantial English-speaking demographic, it is recommended that exhibits include both English and French explanations.

I’m Bruna Volpe, owner of Verena Café, @verenacafe and I’ve visited the Louvre four times — and each visit brings something new. What has impressed me most every time is the Ancient Egypt section. The mummies, sarcophagi, ritual objects and millennia-old artefacts are absolutely captivating. Everything is so well preserved and beautifully curated — it truly feels like stepping into another era. I also loved the Galerie d’Apollon, a breathtaking golden room with painted ceilings and the French crown jewels — it feels like a hidden mini Versailles within the museum. Every time I return, I enjoy walking around and discovering new areas, but so far, these two remain my absolute favourites.
If it’s your first time, don’t miss the Mona Lisa (yes, it’s smaller than you think, but it’s worth it!), and arrive early — the museum is massive, and planning ahead will make all the difference. The Louvre is well worth spending a few hours in — and even then, you'll want to come back again and again. Towards the end of the day, the sunset is absolutely stunning — perfect for photos and enjoying the moment. Definitely a place everyone should visit at least once in their life

The Louvre is more than a museum; it's a magnificent journey through human creativity. Housed in a breathtaking palace, where history whispers from every stone, it safeguards humanity's most precious dreams.
To stand before the Winged Victory, the Venus de Milo, and the enigmatic Mona Lisa is to feel a direct connection to the genius of ages. The vast collections, from ancient civilizations to classical masterpieces, create an overwhelming sense of wonder.
It is a place where art and time intertwine, leaving every visitor profoundly moved by the enduring beauty of our shared story.

The Louvre Museum, located in the heart of Paris, is one of the world’s most famous and visited museums. Here are some interesting facts about this iconic institution:

In 1190, King Philip Augustus built the Louvre as a fortress. In the 14th century, Charles V transformed it into a royal residence, and in 1793, during the French Revolution, it became a museum.
The Louvre is also the largest art museum in the world in terms of exhibition space, covering more than 72,735 square meters of galleries. 350000 artifacts, just 10% on display.
Perhaps the most famous artwork in the museum, Leonardo da Vinci’s Mona Lisa, attracts millions of visitors each year. Her mysterious smile continues to fascinate spectators.
Inaugurated in 1989, the Louvre Pyramid, designed by architect Ieoh Ming Pei, has become an iconic entrance to the museum. Although controversial at the time of its construction, it is now considered a work of art in its own right.
The Louvre's collections span art and artifacts from antiquity to the mid-19th century, divided into eight different departments, including Egyptian, Greek, Etruscan, and Roman antiquities, paintings, sculptures, decorative arts, and more.
During Napoleon Bonaparte’s reign, the museum was renamed the Napoleon Museum, and its collection was significantly enriched by Napoleon’s military campaigns across Europe. He also kept the Mona Lisa in his bedroom.
Over the years, the Louvre has returned artworks to various countries following claims over objects acquired during colonial periods or by questionable means.
This iconic painting by Eugène Delacroix depicts the July Revolution of 1830 in France and is one of the many significant works on display at the Louvre.
The Louvre Museum has been the backdrop for numerous films and television productions, including the famous “Da Vinci Code,” based on Dan Brown’s novel.

Be 30 minutes before your reserved time as it is quite a challenge to find your queue. You can use boxes for your coats and bags inside. For the first visit you definitely can use route from official site. To summarize, it worth to visit. Photos are of art exhibits in the museum.

One of the world's great museums. The Louvre showcases art from the medieval ages to the time of the camera. Countless works of art and masterpieces fill the halls and galleries.

From the Mona Lisa to the Venus de Milo to Winged Victory...there is so much to see. It is worth having a guide for your first visit to the Louvre.

The Louvre is simply unforgettable. From the Mona Lisa to the Venus de Milo, every corner is filled with art and history that feels larger than life. The architecture itself is stunning — the glass pyramid against the historic palace is a view you’ll never forget.
I came from London via the Eurostar, and it was surprisingly easy — just a short walk from Gare du Nord to the Louvre by metro or taxi. If you start early, a day trip is totally doable, though you’ll wish you had more time to explore! Comfortable shoes are a must, and booking tickets in advance saves you from the queues.

Whether you’re an art lover or just curious, this is one of those “must-see once in your life” places.
