On a weekday afternoon after 5:00, the crowds aren’t so bad. Our tickets were for the 7:00 time slot but they let us in at 5:15 with no line whatsoever. We walked right up to the Mona Lisa and took photos with her.

A place must visit.
Be prepared to walk for many hours, bring water, shoes confortables. You can spend all day inside the museum. There are a lot of things to see.

The Louvre Museum is without a doubt one of the most extraordinary cultural experiences in Paris—if not the world. It’s home to an absolutely massive collection of artworks, sculptures, and artifacts from all corners of history, including iconic pieces like the Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, and Liberty Leading the People. But what truly blew me away was the Ancient Assyrian section—such a powerful, often-overlooked part of the museum that deserves more attention. The detail and preservation of those artifacts are incredible.

That said, the line to get in is extremely long, and it can easily eat up hours of your day if you’re not prepared. I’d highly recommend booking tickets in advance, or better yet, joining a guided tour that allows you to skip the line. It makes a huge difference. Also, keep in mind the museum is closed one day a week, so check the schedule ahead of time to avoid disappointment.

Give yourself at least half a day here, ideally more. There’s so much to see and take in—it’s an overwhelming but beautiful experience. With just a bit of planning, it’ll be one of the most rewarding stops on your trip to Paris.

The Louvre is huge and definitely worth spending a whole day exploring. I highly recommend booking the earliest time slot possible, as it will be less crowded around the most popular artworks. The museum itself is beautiful and truly stunning — you can really appreciate the immense manpower, cost, and energy that went into creating this masterpiece.

Make sure you buy your tickets online for the official website. That’s the website of the. Louvre. Even though you have your tickets getting in line pretty early because they segregate lines as per your booking time. In other words will be a line for 11:30am. A new line for 12 noon , another line for 12:30pm aligned for 1pm etc these lines can be 2 to 300 people but once it’s your turn to move in, they do move pretty quickly. It’s just the Security that slows you down. When walking around do the Mona Lisa First it’s the longest queue and you get a lot of pushy people and you will get to the front in about 20 minutes.The rest of the museum is very interesting and broken up into exhibitions. I found the Egyptian exhibition very interesting the jewellery that was worn by the Kings is very cool as well. You definitely cannot do this museum in half a day. Plan to be here for a full day if you want to see everything.. unless you just one of these people that walk through it for the sake of saying you’ve been there.

You cannot miss this experience. It is a labyrinth of amazing wonders. You will get lost. Bring energy you will need to do many steps and stairs. There will be a line to get in at your reserved time but the line moves fast and is managed properly. Wish we had a couple days to spend inside.

It was beautiful I spent a little under half a day there and I really enjoyed myself. The entire museum was beautiful but my favorite part was definitely the fine art upstairs. You can get free tickets on the website (if you meet certain requirements) but be sure to get them early whether the same day or a couple of days before if you’re going during the busy season.

Went on Sunday morning February 16th 2025. The line was pretty long at the mall entrance but breezed through in half hour.

I was debating whether Sunday would be a good day to visit considering the crowd maybe more. But I liked my visit. The Monalisa room was not as crowded as I saw in some photos and videos. Maybe it was the winter season.

The Egyptian collection, the royal rooms, designer’s exhibits, the room with all the jewels- crowns, the popular exhibits with the statues carved out of marble. Loved them all.

I’m glad I was finally able to make it to the Louvre.

We at cafe Angelina inside the louvre. The food and service were both great. I had the ravioli with mushroom. They also had a good IPA. Wine collection is decent.

Very crowded and ridiculously long queues, not just to enter the museum but also for toilets or food. The collection is undoubtedly one of the best in the world, but many rooms and hallways were crammed with people trying to get close to the highlights and get selfies.

The Louvre Museum is massive and full of history.
Every room has something surprising, from ancient statues to famous paintings.
The Mona Lisa is smaller than you expect, but the crowd around it is huge.
There is a secret underground medieval fortress beneath the Louvre is part of the original Louvre from the 12th century.
An amazing building full of incredible works of art.  Essential to buy tickets in advance and plan what you want to see.

The best museum in the world. It has a bit from every country across the world. One would need atleast 2 days to see this marvel in full and in detail. A must visit if you are interested in history. A must visit if you are in Paris. Keep 2 days as reserve for this masterpiece.

Highly recommend to book timed entry tickets in advance. Also have a plan in place and a list of must sees if you want to cover the museum in an organized way. We only had a couple of hours but we were able to make the most of it because we had a good plan. The museum is spectacular and magnanimous. Over 35000 exhibit display across 3 wings. We really enjoyed it a lot. It’s a must see in Paris. The Mona Lisa was stunning .

Older post, but this beautiful gem of a place is as immaculate as one could possibly imagine. 🇫🇷 Didn’t catch ole Mona 🖼️ this time, though here’s hoping next time. Was super awesome to catch the Olympics symbol displayed for what was the 2024 summer Olympics. 🙌🏻

Absolutely worth the visit! It definitely deserves the hype and prestige around it. Beautiful building and some of the most incredible artworks in the world.
I would recommend to get the first hour visit tickets since people are only allowed to enter every half an hour and by 11am it was pretty full and even hard to move around the museum.

Must see attraction. Recommended getting the audio guide as it not only provides interesting facts but a map with your location, which comes in handy as the Louvre is MASSIVE. It will take more than 1-2 days to see it all. We went at 11:30 and there was a long line to get in, go to the carousel entrance the line is a lot shorter, but upon exit at around 3 the line was much shorter.

"There too much - let me sum up" It was amazing!!! I booked my 9am spot, got there 20 minutes before, waited about 30 minutes in line. Saw the Gallerie d' Apollon first - when it was nearly empty. Incredible. Then made my way to the Mona Lisa - waited my turn to get to the front (not so terrible; I am patient) and stood in front of it (relatively small but a masterpiece). Then I slowly meandered through most the rest of the museum. I took lots of pictures of what was inspiring to me. I am astounded at what art and history humans created in this world (especially how badly we can also behave to each other, to other living beings, and to the planet). Left the museum after 7pm - long, long day but completely astounding and fascinating.

I joined small group tour this time and much enjoyable than I visited on my own. My kids also put this place as his number one during our stay thanks to our guide.
It helps me understand the historical background and the arts along with all of authentic artefacts.
Should have gone earlier in the day! This place is immense. Full of incredible artifacts and an abundance of history. We even saw the monalisa!

A dream come true – The Louvre is beyond words!

Finally visiting the Louvre was the fulfillment of a lifelong dream. I had imagined this moment for years, and being here in person exceeded all expectations. You could spend years inside and still not see everything – it’s that vast, rich, and endlessly fascinating.

From ancient wonders to iconic masterpieces like the Mona Lisa, the energy, the history, the scale of it all… it left me speechless. Every room is a portal through time, every corner tells a story.

This isn’t just a museum – it’s a world of its own. I left feeling inspired, humbled, and deeply grateful. If you love art, history, or simply being in awe, this place will stay with you forever.

Was lovely.
The staff were great and happy to help.

Would definitely recommend booking a time slot in advance.

When you are going into different sections you will be asked to see your tickets again.

As you may imagine there was a long que for the Mona Lisa, but other than that everything was easily assessable.

Would totally recommend it if you're in Paris.

Time wise we didn't rush and we didn't see everything but were there for about 2 to 3 hours.

The largest museum in the world!
It houses an incredible collection of around 380,000 artifacts.

Its vast collection includes iconic pieces like the Mona Lisa and the Venus de Milo.
The architecture itself is stunning, blending history with modern design.

I purchased my Louvre Museum ticket a month before my trip, which made the entry process smooth and hassle-free.
Plan your visit well, as it can be crowded, but every moment spent here is worth it.

The Louvre is absolutely breathtaking, but its massive size can be overwhelming for a family. We decided to focus on a few key wings, and it was a wonderful experience! Seeing masterpieces like the Mona Lisa and the Winged Victory of Samothrace up close was unforgettable. Surprisingly, our daughter was totally fascinated by the grand galleries and the ancient Egyptian collection. The iconic glass pyramid outside is spectacular and makes for incredible family photos. It requires a lot of walking, but it is an absolute masterpiece of a museum. Highly recommend booking tickets and planning your route ahead of time!

I live in Rome, which is also a very touristy city, but I honestly didn’t expect such poor organization from the Louvre. We bought tickets for 11:00 a.m., arrived at 10:45, and only got inside at 12:20. All that time we were standing in line just to enter so buying a ticket for a specific time doesn’t guarantee admission at all.

We visited just one day before the jewelry theft at the Louvre , it’s absurd how they manage security and send guards in the wrong direction. We used the side entrance (which is supposed to be less crowded than the main one through the pyramid), yet we still waited for an hour and a half. The security check system is terrible , only one staff member and one scanner for such a massive crowd. Meanwhile, the thieves somehow managed to sneak in through an outside staircase.

Inside, it was just as chaotic, way too many people and absolutely no organization. I’ve worked as a tour guide in Rome, so I know what crowds are like, and I was prepared for that. But what I saw at the Louvre was just a complete failure of organization.

Buy tickets on line is the easier way. It is super crowded but still orderly. There are 3 main wings covering paintings and sculptures. If you don't have the whole day for this museum and wants to make sure you see Mona Lisa painting, go to "Denon" Hall right away. Same direction if you are keen only on paintings from France and Italy. It takes easily 1.5 to 2 hrs in this wing. The other 2 halls are mainly sculptures and antiques from reknown artists and sculptors. Good to engage a guide or get audio guide in advance so that there are highlights and commentaries making the visit more meaningful.

The Louvre, largest museum in the world, 9 million visitors a year, #1 on every list, you’re gonna go. It was everything they said it was. The statues, the paintings, the Mona Lisa, all amazing. That being said, still, lower your expectations a little bit. It will be packed, there will be long, long, lines.

Seeing the Mona Lisa was like going to a concert, that everyone else went to, and waiting to meet the band in an ocean of people. When you look at a photo of the Mona Lisa, look at the reflection of the protective shield, you’ll see the sea of people.

If you go during the summer, be prepared to be hot, yes they have AC, so you won’t die, but you will sweat. A lot of people were turning their maps into fans. If you’re expecting a cool cafe, surprise, it’s a Starbucks.

Yes, it’s huge, maybe do two days if you want to see everything. Go back at night after closing, and take a nice walk outside, the grounds are beautiful. All that being said, easily the greatest museum on the planet. 5 Stars, they’ve earned it.

A bit confusing to get around with the maps. Could improve.
Exhibitions were nice and informative.
The Mona Lisa is crowded, always.

Visiting the Louvre Museum was an unforgettable experience! The collection is massive, and there’s so much to see that it can be overwhelming. That’s why I highly recommend going with a guide. Our guide brought the art and history to life with interesting stories and helped us find the highlights, like the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo, without getting lost. I learned so much more than I would have on my own, and it made the visit much more enjoyable and meaningful. If you want to get the most out of the Louvre, definitely consider a guided tour!

Loved it! The emplacement, the art, the easy to get in and around, the order inside, the cleanness. It s a very crowded place even on Weekdays. World's biggest museum so you have to visit it when in Paris. Worth the visit because there are some world class works of art here. Mona Lisa is the most popular art piece in this museum and it will always be crowded to view it. But plenty of signs leading you to exactly where this is located.

It’s a beautiful walk w/shaded trees and places to sit on benches even bring food or buy and spend a wonderful day enjoying their temperate weather.

A 5 all day due to the history and art.

A Must-See, but Plan Wisely!

The Louvre is absolutely worth visiting — no question about it. But if you go in summer, be ready for crowds.

☝️Tips from my visit:
• Buy tickets online in advance (ideally for the morning).
• Arrive 30 minutes early.
• Bring your own water (inside, prices will eat you alive).
• Plan ahead which masterpieces you want to see first (Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, Winged Victory).
• Travel light — no big bags, but free lockers are available.

Yes, the Mona Lisa has huge crowds (most just want a quick photo), but the Louvre is so much more than that. Don’t miss the Egyptian antiquities, Mesopotamian treasures, French paintings, and Napoleon’s Apartments — all just as impressive, with fewer people.

The Louvre isn’t just a museum, it’s another world.
We only spent a couple of hours but we found it amazing. We enjoyed it thoroughly and hope to return

Key highlight is probably Mona Lisa but there are plenty of other famous artworks around. This place is huge and i highly recommend the audio guide. Part of the museum pass but remember to reserve your time slot so that you can get in.

The Louvre is absolutely stunning so much history, art, and culture in one place. You definitely need at least 4–5 hours to really take it in. It was crowded, but the staff were all very helpful and guided us through it. A must visit in Paris.

Totally worth the visit, but be prepared to walk for more than 4 hours if you want to see everything it has to offer. A lot of different cultures, time periods and artifacts. One of the best museums I’ve ever visited!

Buy the tickets in advance and be there 10mins before your time slot!
I didn’t think I’d enjoy this but I loved it! Definitely too large to see even half of it in a day! Be sure to get your tickets online before you go!

The Louvre is an absolutely stunning museum that truly requires several days to explore thoroughly. We visited for a Couture exhibition that was located in a more remote part of the museum, and it was absolutely fantastic to see. Finding Napoleon’s apartments proved to be a bit of a challenge, but once we did, it was absolutely worth the effort. The sheer scale and richness of the collection are overwhelming in the best way possible—an unforgettable experience for any art and history lover!
Truly beautiful but busy museum. Many tourists flock here to see the famous art pieces. Didn’t stay for very long as we were in a rush and had places to go but it is definitely worth a visit.

Definitely worth a visit. I saw a lot of beautiful paintings from the 15th, 16th, and 17th centuries by Northern European artists which I particularly enjoyed. The downside is that it does get very hot inside the museum and being popular it's very crowded. Possibly not somewhere I would return to next time, but I would really recommend a visit.

The Louvre is a must-visit destination. I recommend to book the 9 AM time slot and aim to arrive about 30 minutes early. There's usually a queue for ticket scanning, so being there ahead of time ensures a smooth start to your experience.

Visited midweek, extremely busy. Well worth booking tickets online before visit. Audio guide very difficult to understand, not really worth the money. If you have a blue badge you can jump the queue with priority entry . No need to queue to see Mona Lisa either,  disabled are allowed priority access. So much to see ,allow yourself at least 4 - 5 hours to look around.

What can one say about such an extensive and iconic museum. Although it was extremely busy the large size of the Museum allows you to find your own space when you need it. Would highly recommend you set aside a large amount of time if you plan to see all of it. We were there for four and a half hours and still only made it through one entire floor. The art and history is really unlike anywhere. Every aspect of it from the building to the art on display gives you something to marvel at. We ate at the cafe and although the line was long we were pleasantly surprised how quickly we made it through. The meal was just what we needed and prices were what you’d expect for the convenience of eating in the Museum. All and all an unforgettable experience. Highly recommend you buy tickets in advance!

Amazing Museum, so much to see so plan your day well. Research in advance areas you're interested in. My favourite was seeing all the beautiful artifacts in the Egyptian section. Not sure when I'll actually visit a pyramid but was great to experience entering a reconstructed slice of a pyramid. An experience I would never forget. I will have to revisit, as didn't get to see a couple of sections including the French Royal jewels.

Visited the Louvre Medium and absolutely loved it! Even though it can get a bit crowded, spending 7–8 hours there was totally worth it. The art pieces are incredible. Highly recommend getting the audio guide, especially the 3D version—it really enhances the experience. A must-visit for art lovers!

The biggest museum on earth, the most iconic museum, stand for French. You should never miss to visit here whenever you are in Paris. Make sure to book the ticket up front.

So, so big. This museum is very huge and very confusing even with the map. Although, the rooms very clearly marked, we still had to work hard to find our way around. There are plenty of things to see, beautiful art from every era and every place you can think of.

At the beginning we waited in a very long line but thankfully the whole process was very organised and fast. Be warned that the most famous pieces will be crowded no matter what. We would highly recommend Napoleon III rooms on the second floor.

What we appreciated the most was the cloakroom which was self serving and we could come there as we pleased. Food in the museum was overpriced but not bad or any special.

Overall make sure you make enough time to wander around and appreciate what it has to offer.

What a beauty it is. One of the best. Seems

It’s really one of my favourite museums in the world. Every time we are in Paris, we go there. Yes, the lines can be annoying and the instructions could be a bit clear - but the art more than makes up for it. There is something about liberty leading the people that makes it all worth it. If you want to have a shorter line, you could go to the mall next to it.

Must visit place in Paris. So much of art and so much to see. The pics are from my second visit to this beautiful city in winters and the weather was cold but enjoyable. It took almost a full day to see the museum but it was time well spent. I'll suggest one to go through the info pamphlets and make a route tour for saving time. Read about the French history and historical events to make the best of the visit and prepare your kids for a long day if planning to take along.
Worth the wait and worth the $. A must visit even if you only go to see the Italian and French painting hallways

Excellent museum with state-of-the-art facilities and impressive architecture. It includes many departments and collections. I was impressed by the ancient Greek and Hellenistic period exhibits as well as Delacroix's paintings. At least 2 to 3 hours are required for a tour. Unfortunately the maps didn't help much and the heat was unbearable. I would like to admire those ancient Greek masterpieces in their place of origin and not in European museums. Ticket cost 22€.

the museum is gorgeous and gigantic! the amount of art and history that is in one location is incredible. we got there 10 minutes before our timed tickets and waited for about 15 minutes before getting into the museum. people who got there at the time of ticket or after had to wait much longer. so make sure to go a little early. once inside, they have free lockers to put keep your belongings while you walk around. the reason why i subtracted a star was because of how crowded it was to see Mona Lisa. there is no order when heading to the room to see Mona Lisa, there is a very specific area for viewing and it was extremely crowded. staff does not guide people to move and there is no time limit to how long you can spend so you either have to be patient or shove your way to the front. i wish there was a better process and more efficiency when it comes to viewing the Mona Lisa. they needed to keep the crowd moving along so everyone can get a chance to view. other than that, i really enjoyed the museum and a whole day could have been spent there.

The Louvre, a former palace and now a museum cannot be compared to anything else in the world.

(Definitely buy the tickets online to avoide even longer queues)

Definitely the symbol of Paris. The Louvre is a must-visit and worth every euro. It’s best to buy tickets online to avoid the long lines. Don’t waste too much time waiting just to see the Mona Lisa – there are so many other masterpieces that deserve your attention.
no matter how many times you come back, you will always discover something new - a magical place

Here you will find some of the most amazing paintings, sculptures, & artistic wonders of the entire world.

The Louvre is a breathtaking blend of history, art, and architecture. Everyone just rushes to entering the museum and then leaves. Seine is on your left. And you can see Eiffel Tower standing far end to the left too. You can admire the building of Louvre along the street, which is as grand as the inside of Louvre.
The museum closed on Tuesday. My hotel is just nearby so I was there. It was so nice without any crowds for the photos taken.
Mind you even you have booked online for your ticket. One still needs to wait 10 mins queue to get in. And a pair of walking shoes !

A museum that is worth visiting at least once in a lifetime. Lots of works of all kinds, paintings, vases, sculptures, decorations, etc.
This time we bought the tickets: it could happen that the visit is free, but in any case the price of the ticket considering the size of the museum is reasonable.

The Louvre is a must-visit in Paris! Stunning architecture, an incredible art collection, and, of course, the Mona Lisa. The museum is massive, so plan ahead. Crowds can be heavy, but it’s worth it. A perfect place for art and history lovers—truly an unforgettable experience!

It’s a bit of a wait and you for sure need to hook a ticket 2 weeks or more in advance. I wouldn’t bother with the audio tour and try to avoid a weekend. They have amazing artifacts back to early Roman times and obviously the Mona Lisa. It’s worth going if in Paris!

The greatest museum I have ever seen in Europe. You can see so many different arts.

Prepare to spend the entire day in the museum. You need about 6-7 hours to see all the museum. You can find coffee shops inside, or bring something to eat with you

You have to visit at least once in your life. We came just 3-4 hours before closing time thinking that would be sufficient but this museum is huge and there's so much to see and learn about. To say their collection is vast is truly an understatement. Seeing the artworks they had in real life was breathtaking. If you're someone who appreciates art and history, I recommend spending an entire day here. They also have a great gift shop, there's a souvenir for everyone. Will definitely visit again.

The Louvre is a breathtaking museum filled with history, art, and an incredible atmosphere. Its stunning architecture and vast collection, including masterpieces like the Mona Lisa, make it a must-visit. Walking through its halls, you can feel the aura of centuries-old paintings and sculptures. However, it takes time to enter due to long lines, so buying tickets in advance is highly recommended. Inside, the crowds can be overwhelming, but the experience is absolutely worth it!

Unique place! It’s probably the only museum in the world where you can see European paintings and sculptures, an Egyptian mummy and sarcophagus, and prehistoric Islamic artifacts all in one location.

We spent six hours there, and there was still so much left to see.

The building is enormous, making it easy to get lost. I highly recommend buying tickets in advance and planning exactly what you want to see.

The Louvre is an incredible museum. We took a guided tour showing the top 10 or so pieces and our guide gave us an in depth view of the history of the piece. Way too much to see here in a visit, so plan your visit to see what you really want.

It is kinda ridiculous to review and especially to rate the Louvre, one of the best museums in the world. Of course, the collection is spectacular and comprehensive! A couple of practical considerations, though. It is impossible to see the whole collection in one single day (unless you are running through the halls not registering much). Unfortunately, the museum is very overcrowded, so it is crucial to buy tickets in advance (actually, I think that now it is the only option). Even with the reservation, there will be a significant line to enter. Carousel entrance is considered a better option in terms of waiting time, but in my experience the line there was still huge. Inside, the collection is scattered between 3 wings. To enter each wing you have to pass through the separate ticket control, which is inconvenient taking into account the crowds. If you want to limit your visit to only one wing, you may choose Denon, where the most famous paintings, including Mona Lisa, are located (to see her  it is recommended to come as early as possible to be able to get close). The audioguide is good, but I found the Nintendo device they use cumbersome and heavy.

Louvre is fantastic. It's also huge, and full of crowds, and it's disorienting. We didn't properly prepare for our visit, researching which rooms to give priority to, and so spent a lot of time feeling lost. Next time I would do my homework first. You'd probably need at least 3 visits to fully explore everything.
One of the best (if not the best) museums I have visited. A lot to see here, and one caneasily spend a whole day looking at the exhibit. The architecture and interior decor are simply breathtaking.

It was great, the museum closed at 6, it is recommended to get tickets for the morning, and you should buy tickets a week in advance so they don't run out. Strollers were rented for children to access.

An astonishing collection of art. Always crowded so get a timed entry ticket and be there a few minutes early.
The museum can be challenging to navigate if you have any physical disabilities or difficulties with steps. The elevators are sometimes hidden and some only go to a floor or two. Get a map. Ask the docents for directions. Take a cane or walking stick. Seating is also very limited.
Food in the cafe and restaurant was good. The wait was only a few minutes.

Great attraction and a popular museum famous worldwide. Despite visiting Paris on three separate occasions, I am yet to visit the museum itself due to the waiting crowds!
The Louvre and the area surrounding it is rich with great architecture and creative structures. There is still a lot to be enjoyed even if you are unable to visit the museum itself. It is located right in the middle of Paris near famous parks and places in the city. If you do not have the opportunity to visit Paris more than once in your lifetime, I would recommend that you visit the museum and its surrounding areas when in this beautiful city.

We went out of the season, and it was still very crowded but worth it! Most beautiful art pieces are on display and not to even start with the majestic art all over the ceilings. It is absolutely majestic and worth a bit of time in a queue.

You really need to take a few days to visit this place. It is so vast and there is so much to see and experience. One day is certainly not enough and if one day is all you have, plan ahead of time exactly what it is you want to visit, even then you will get to stop at the most beautiful art and exhibitions imaginable.

Louvre Museum definitely worth the visit! We managed to buy the tickets from Get Your Guide . Apparently, the staff said no tickets available on site. It was fully booked until May 2025. Queue going in the museum was long and it was crowded inside too. Especially crowded visiting to see the famous Mona Lisa picture. Glad we were visited the museum!

It’s the most worthwhile museum to visit in Paris, open until 9 p.m. on Wednesdays and Fridays. You can’t pay directly on the official website, so tickets have to be bought through online agents, and if you try to book a week in advance, only entry slots after 12 p.m. are available. Visiting for eight hours means covering over ten kilometers on foot, but it’s absolutely worth it—this museum has the richest collection of art in the world!

Pretty cool. Make sure to get tickets ahead of time. The line was long to get in but once they opened the doors for our time slot, it went by quick . It’s pretty hot and stuffy inside. I was regretting wearing a sweater .

We were impressed but not surprised by the amazing artwork they had.

Awesome experience. If you plan on exploring the entire museum in 1 day, prepare to be disappointed. My advice would be to prioritize the exhibits you want to see and map those out accordingly. It's literally impossible to see everything in one visit, so if you must, then plan on multiple visits.

Enormous. More steps than I have even seen in any museum, great exercise! Filled to the brim with artefacts.
I was hoping to see an Egyptian collection to rival the British museum, it did not.
The map does not even cover all of the rooms.

Something to do at least once in your life.

But if you want to do it entirely it needs a few days.

The audio phone is also very useful.

Wow. An absolutely stunning place to go. I spent so much time absorbing the grounds outside the Louvre, and the ceilings detail inside, that I can’t even tell you if I looked at more than a couple actual art pieces at all. Ok, the Mona Lisa and a couple sculptures. Honestly, it was more than enough to scour the glorious architecture and sculpted motifs, the ceiling art, and the design of the actual rooms - there was NO WAY I could focus on the framed art as well.
Take your time, but plan in advance which pieces are most important for you to see, and let yourself be swept away by the most magnificent beauty and opulence you’ve ever experienced. (Unless you’re royalty, and already live in a Palace. Then I guess you’ll be jaded, and the art will be more interesting to you.)
Visiting the Louvre felt like stepping into the heart of art and history. They call it the best museum in the world for a reason! My second time at the Louvre and certainly not the last! Love it

We were able to get in and see a great Michelangelo Rodin sculpture exhibition. But, we had issues with tickets they were under a different name and could not see all...  Be aware of ticket companies.  Buy directly from the Louvre website

Such a beautiful place to visit!  You need time to take in everything,  so book ahead so that you have a morning entry and can take your time exploring the museum.  You wait in this line about 15 minutes ahead of your timed entry.   It was raining the day i went and so there were vendors selling umbrellas.  Bargain with them to get a fair price!  I would say it is a bit confusing getting around inside even with a map, so I would ask the staff for help if lost.   I didn't get an audio tour, but might be a good idea if you have a whole day.  But, I was able to explore and read the descriptions found next to artwork and that was good enough.
Plan to spend at least a couple of days here if you really want to take in all the art. It’s easy to see why the Louvre is so famous.

Being one of the largest, oldest, and grandest museums in the world, the Louvre hosts a treasure trove of artworks spanning diverse cultures and periods of history. The main attractions at the museum are the Mona Lisa, the Venus de Milo, Winged Victory, and stunning collections of prized paintings and Greek and Roman statues.

If you have enough time and budget, I recommend splitting your visit to the Musée du Louvre over several days.
Immerse yourself in the great works of art!

Get tickets way before your trip. It’s always going to be crowded I visited on a Thursday morning and it was very busy even w a 9 am ticket it was a big queue. Plan to see other works than the big paintings as well. You may see more that way. Make sure to do the Pyramid entry.

We arrived at the Louvre on Wednesday, having bought tickets in advance. However, there were still so many people that we had to wait around 30-40 minutes for everyone to be checked and allowed in. Unfortunately, we didn’t manage to visit or explore all the galleries because the museum is so vast that you definitely need a couple of days to fully experience it. Plus, the large crowds create constant noise, which makes it hard to fully enjoy the atmosphere. But this is a place you absolutely must see at least once in your life. The beauty is unreal, and it’s incredible how talented people are to create such masterpieces!

No words to describe the beauty of this museum. The best and most beautiful museum in the world contains art pieces that'll blow your mind. You should visit this museum at least once in your lifetime. And it is a very big museum so pre book your tickets a week in advance and have a complete day for your visit.

Great museum! I recommend buying the tickets online before the visit and choosing the early morning to avoid long queues!
I've entered the museum at 8:30 and I was able to enter at 9:10 by the underground access to the Carrousel center.

My family and I joined the Louvre Museum Masterpieces Guided Tour with Reserved Access, and it was absolutely worth it! 🎨 Our guide was fantastic — knowledgeable, engaging, and full of interesting stories that brought each masterpiece to life. We skipped the long lines and went straight in, which saved a lot of time ⏱️. The highlights like the Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, and Winged Victory were incredible to see in person. 🖼️ The museum is huge, so having a guide made it much easier to navigate and focus on the most famous pieces. Highly recommend this tour if you want a stress-free and memorable Louvre experience! 🇫🇷✨
